cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Steve Rowson thinks of the newer football helmets. The options are A through D. First, I need to find where Steve Rowson is mentioned in the text. Let me scan through the passage again. The text says, "Steve Rowson is an engineer at Virginia Tech. In his lab, he tests how helmets hold up against different types of hits. No helmet can prevent concussions completely, says Rowson. But he's found that when wearing a newer football helmet, the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. That lowers the risk of concussions." So, the key points here are that he's an engineer testing helmets. He says newer helmets make the player's head not gain speed as much after a hit, which lowers concussion risk. The options are about what he thinks of them. Looking at the options: A) They vary a lot in size. The text doesn't mention size variation. B) They work better than the old ones. The passage says newer helmets reduce the speed gain of the head, implying they're more effective. C) They slow down the player's running speed. The text mentions the head not gaining speed as much after a hit, not the player's running speed. D) They allow the players to move their heads more freely. The passage doesn't discuss freedom of movement. So the correct answer should be B. The newer helmets work better than
